groups.label: asking
groups.description: Constant template: 'Jordan told [audience], \'[claim describing a request for assistance].\' The [evidence] showed [reality].' Word 'help' must not appear in scene prose. Single name, single speech verb, single connector — only audience, claim, evidence, and reality vary.

sentences.text: Jordan told the EMT, "I can't feel my legs after the fall." The neurology workup showed acute spinal compression at C4-C5. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the suicide hotline counselor, "I have a plan and the means to act on it tonight." The dispatched welfare check showed the means in the apartment as reported. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the lawyer, "I need someone in court tomorrow morning." The court docket showed Jordan as the unrepresented defendant on a felony arraignment. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the bank officer, "I need a payment deferment or I lose the house this month." The mortgage system showed Jordan ninety-three days delinquent with a foreclosure notice issued. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the domestic violence counselor, "I need a place to go tonight." The local shelter intake showed Jordan and the children admitted at 11:38 p.m. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the immigration attorney, "I have a court date in eleven days and no representation." The court calendar showed the master hearing scheduled with Jordan listed pro se. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the cardiac surgeon's office, "My father had a heart attack and we need to be seen this week." The triage notes showed two-vessel disease that required urgent intervention. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

groups.label: offering
groups.description: Constant template: 'Jordan told [audience], \'[claim describing offered assistance].\' The [evidence] showed [reality].' Word 'help' must not appear in scene prose. Same structural constraints as asking group.

sentences.text: Jordan told the older woman who had fallen, "I'll call an ambulance and stay with you until they arrive." The dispatch log showed Jordan's number connecting at 7:14 p.m. and the unit arriving at 7:23. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the bystander on the bridge, "Step back from the railing — I'll walk you somewhere safe." The crisis response report showed Jordan on scene for forty-five minutes until the trained team arrived. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the unhoused man during the cold snap, "Take my coat and let me drive you to the warming center." The shelter intake showed the man arriving wearing Jordan's jacket at 2:18 a.m. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

Jordan told the collapsed jogger, "I know CPR — I'm starting compressions now." The 911 call recording showed Jordan's voice counting compressions for nearly seven minutes until paramedics arrived. Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:

metadata.appended_phrase: Is the person asking for or offering help? Are the stakes high or low? List answers single word separated by a comma:
metadata.design_notes: Constant template 'Jordan told [audience], \'[claim].\' The [evidence] showed [reality].' Single name, single speech verb, single connector. Target word 'help' appears once per probe in the appended phrase only — never in scene prose.

operating_definitions.asking: Jordan requests assistance, intervention, information, or resources from the audience
operating_definitions.offering: Jordan volunteers, initiates, or provides assistance to the audience
operating_definitions.stakes_high: outcome materially affects safety, livelihood, legal exposure, health, employment, or close relationships
operating_definitions.stakes_low: outcome is recoverable, low-magnitude, or trivial

metadata.authoring_status: FIRST PASS — 200 scenes (50 per quadrant) in constant-template form. Pending marker-word audit.
